page: Microsoft Azure E16d_v5 is an EDv5 series virtual machine configured with 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The underlying hardware features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz, containing 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. This dedicated CPU allocation includes a cache hierarchy of 48 KB L1d, 1 MB L2, and 48 MB L3 cache. The server is equipped with 614 GB of local storage, while no GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results position this instance in the average performance tier across most standardized tests. The system achieved a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1899.39 and a multi-core score of 15536.33. Memory performance tests show a read-only small block bandwidth of 834.28 GB/sec and a large block write bandwidth of 56.93 GB/sec. In database operations, the server processed 14.9 million Redis SET operations per second and scored 8058.61 on PassMark database operations. Large language model inference benchmarks show a strong text generation speed of 261.72 tokens per second for a small 135M model, though performance scales down to 11.63 tokens per second for a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E16d_v5 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core alongside bundled local storage, making it a resource-dense option for memory-intensive workloads without requiring separate storage attachments. Given the 128.0 GB memory capacity and local disk, this server is suited for relational database management systems, caching layers, data indexing, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E16d_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the EDv5 family, featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, it includes 8 physical cores, a 48 MB L3 cache, and 614 GB of local storage, with no GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 9964.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 14.9 million operations per second. It demonstrates strong text generation capabilities on small LLM models at 261.72 tokens per second. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core, this server provides a balanced, resource-dense profile for memory-intensive workloads. It is well-suited for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16d_v5 is an EDv5 virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16d_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage running on Intel Xeon processors.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, high memory density, and integrated local storage for database workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
128.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8 GB per core memory-to-CPU ratio
614 GB of integrated local storage for fast data access
Solid database performance with 14.9 million Redis SET operations per second
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, caching, and enterprise applications
